// Module
var code = "<p>What would be the controlling limit state if the tension member was a WT6x25?</p> <hr/> <p>WTs are made by cutting a wide-flange section lengthwise, either in a dedicated shop or at the jobsite itself. They are rarely, if ever, hot-rolled as separate sections. The cut can be achieved in a variety of ways. Due to residual stresses in the W section, the two pieces will actually bend and may need to be straightened:</p> <figure> <img src=./bending-of-tee-when-cut.gif alt=\"\"/><figcaption>Bending of tee as the source W section is cut. [<a href=\"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=x3qNUgCmbDY\">Source</a>]</figcaption> </figure> <hr/> <p>The block shear pattern with <a href=#Block-shear-1>tension in the flange and shear in the stem</a> is a bit out of the ordinary. The AISC <em>Engineering Journal</em> published papers that discuss this sort of failure in <a href=https://www.aisc.org/Block-Shear-of-Structural-Tees-in-Tension-Alternate-Paths>1996</a> and <a href=https://www.aisc.org/Block-Shear-Net-Section-Capacities-of-Struct-Tees-in-Tension-Test-Results-Code-Implications>2002</a>.</p> <hr/> <p>There are a lot of limit states even for this one connection. If you are unsure which controls, or if you are writing a design spreadsheet, it is a good idea to check them all. With experience, you will gain a feel for which limit states control a given connection and simplify your calculations.</p> <hr/> <p>What is the strength of the bolt group in this connection? Remember to consider bearing and tearout in the connected material as well as bolt shear rupture.</p> <hr/> <p>This connection has eccentricity – the centroid of the tension member is offset from the centroid of the gusset plate. As a result, moments are generated which may need to be considered in design. Historically, eccentricity has been ignored for tension members. It cannot be ignored for compression members.</p> <hr/> <p>Do you have a fun idea to add to this page? Learn how to contribute it in <a href=../docs/#Adding-a-more-fun-item>the documentation</a>.</p> ";
// Exports
module.exports = code;
